data.narrative:
Deep Isolation is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Elizabeth Muller and Richard A. Muller, with $26M in total capital raised to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy firms: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and General Matter.

Stage-progression methodology applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $26M raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $52M. This reflects typical Series A-to-next-stage step-ups within the frontier nuclear sector. Peer-median triangulation was not applicable: while four same-sector peers exist, insufficient datapoints satisfied narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) to generate a reliable median independent of the stage-progression result. Drawing an all-sector median would introduce unwarranted noise given the specialized nature of frontier nuclear technology.

The triangulated valuation range is $44M–$60M, with a median of $52M. This single-method reliance represents a key analytical constraint. Primary uncertainties include technology-stage risk, regulatory pathway clarity, and funding environment dynamics specific to frontier energy. A broader peer dataset at comparable development stages, or forward-looking revenue or deployment milestones, would strengthen confidence in this range. For Series A deployment decisions, this band should be treated as indicative rather than definitive until additional comparables or operational metrics become available.
